rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL JOHN SHANNON VS. WASHINGTON MUTUAL, INC. (DBA WASHINGTON MUTUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 1. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SHANNON'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File SAC is GRANTED. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL JOHN SHANNON VS. WASHINGTON MUTUAL, INC. (DBA WASHINGTON MUTUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Pursuant To Ccp 473
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 1. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SHANNON's Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Pursuant To Ccp 473. Plaintiff's Motion to Set Aside is GRANTED. Plaintiff met the requirements for discretionary relief per CCP 473(b). = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: ROSE OKELLO VS. INDYMAC MBS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens, Supporting Declaration Of Howard Epstein, Supporting Memorandum And Order
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 2. INTERVENOR 168 JERSEY STREET, LLC'S Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens. Intervenor's Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is GRATED. Plaintiff failed to show by preponderance of evidence probable validity of her claims. Intervenor's request for costs and sanctions is grated: $510 in costs and $750 in attorney's fees. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD WESTON VS. TOMMY K ABIB et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 3. DEFENDANT TOMMY ABIB'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to First Aamended Complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to 6th cause of action to allege UCL per Cal-Tech 20 Cal.App.4th 163. Demurer is overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiff has sufficiently pleaded causes of action under CCP 1060. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: SA CHALLENGER, INC., A MINNESOTA CORPORATION VS. DOUGLAS MOODY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 4. Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Complaint. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Arbitration is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff failed to sustain its burden under CCP 1281.2 and plead prior demand for arbitration and refusal to arbitrate. E-mail submitted in Reply does not constitute demand. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: N.R PATEL et al VS. JOSEPH LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint; Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 5. PLAINTIFF PATEL, N.R DBA HENRY HOTEL's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: CINDY OILAR, TRUSTEE OF THE SILVIO L. SCOCCA 1996 VS. ANTHONY BICKEL et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 6. DEFENDANTs ANTHONY BICKEL and KATHERINE PARRICK's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Continued to November 16, 2012. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, VS. LARRY CHEW et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 7. DEFENDANT LARRY CHEW's Motion To Vacate Judgment. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Judgment is Off Calendar, untimely CCP 1005. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL MIDDLE VS. JOHN ROBERT WETTSTEIN et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 13. PLAINTIFF PAUL MIDDLE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted subject to opposition. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: TRUE COMPASS, LLC VS. WENXIU GAO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 8. DEFENDANT WENXIU GAO's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment is GRANTED. Plaintiff fails to provide evidence that Defendant was notified and/or refused to pick up FedEx package. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. GREGORY ORME et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 9. DEFENDANT GREGORY ORME's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Defendant's Motion to Vacate Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain burden under CCP 473(b). = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: MIKHAIL GOLOMB VS. MICHAEL DAVILA et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 10. DEFENDANT MICHAEL DAVILA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is sustained with leave to amend to allege expiration of fixed term tenancy, any amendments to the lease and compliance or exemption from SFRO. Specifically paragraphs 6d, 6e and 14. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M. SAGONOWSKY VS. LANCE WARREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 11. PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Sanctions. Hearing required. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: WEST COAST PROPERTY MANAGEMENT COMPANY VS. DARIO SANMIGUEL et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, November 13, 2012, line 12. DEFENDANT DARIO SANMIGUEL's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's Motion for Summary judgment is GRANTED. Violation of SF Admin. Code 37.3 is not jusct cause for eviction under SFRO per Regulation 6.15C. = (KS/501)

rulings.Case Title: MF FUND II, LP VS. NINOSKA HERNANDEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: No appearances, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF MF FUND II, LP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. is Off Calendar per moving party. Judge Kay Tsenin, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (KS/501)
